context: Tests whether the solver applies swift-security Keychain review rules to an OAuth token helper and leaves gradable evidence in security-review.md.

checklist.name: Plaintext secret
checklist.description: Identifies the refresh token in preferences as insecure persisted secret storage.

checklist.name: Severity stated
checklist.description: Assigns severity to the secret-storage finding and at least one Keychain implementation finding.

checklist.name: OSStatus handling
checklist.description: Requires checking SecItem result codes instead of ignoring them.

checklist.name: Duplicate case
checklist.description: Names duplicate-item handling as part of the write path.

checklist.name: Missing item case
checklist.description: Names missing-item handling for reads, deletes, update-first flows, or tests.

checklist.name: Locked device case
checklist.description: Names interaction-not-allowed or locked-device behavior as a recoverable Keychain case.

checklist.name: Explicit protection
checklist.description: Requires an explicit Keychain accessibility or access-control policy for added items.

checklist.name: No delete update
checklist.description: Rejects delete-before-add as the normal update strategy.

checklist.name: Add or update
checklist.description: Recommends an add-or-update or update-or-add strategy that preserves the item when update succeeds.

checklist.name: Test checklist
checklist.description: Includes tests for success plus at least two failure/edge paths from duplicate, missing item, locked device, logout, or migration.
